metadata.title: Striver's A2Z DSA Sheet
metadata.description: Complete DSA learning roadmap from A to Z - extracted from takeuforward.org
metadata.author: Raj Vikramaditya (Striver)

problems.optimal_approach: Use cin/cout in C++, Scanner in Java, input() in Python
Understand int, long, float, double, char, bool
Conditional branching
Use switch for multiple discrete conditions
Iterate with counter variable
Iterate while condition is true
Use pass-by-reference for modifying values without extra space
Big O, Big Theta, Big Omega notations
Two nested for loops with condition
Two nested for loops
Two nested for loops printing column index
Combine upper and lower halves with space condition
Repeatedly divide by 10
Mod by 10 to extract last digit, build reversed number

problems.problem_name: Check if a Number is Palindrome
problems.brute_force: Convert to string, check if string equals its reverse
problems.optimal_approach: Reverse the number mathematically and compare
Euclidean algorithm: gcd(a,b) = gcd(b, a%b)

problems.brute_force: Extract each digit, compute power, sum, compare
problems.optimal_approach: Same - O(log n) using digit extraction

problems.problem_name: Print All Divisors of a Number
problems.optimal_approach: Iterate 1 to √n, add both i and n/i

problems.brute_force: O(n) - check all numbers from 2 to n-1
problems.optimal_approach: Check divisors only up to √n
Base case + recursive call with increasing index
Recursive call + print after return
f(n) = n + f(n-1), base: f(0)=0
f(n) = n * f(n-1), base: f(0)=1
Recursive: swap(arr[l], arr[r]) then call(l+1, r-1)

problems.problem_name: Check if String is Palindrome using Recursion
problems.optimal_approach: Recursive: if s[l]!=s[r] return false, else check(l+1, r-1)
Memoization/DP: store computed values
Use HashMap/unordered_map for O(1) lookups

problems.brute_force: Sort and scan O(n log n)
problems.optimal_approach: Build frequency map, find max/min

problems.brute_force: N/A - this is the brute approach
problems.optimal_approach: Find minimum in unsorted portion and swap to front
Optimized with early exit if no swaps
Shift elements instead of swapping
Divide in half, sort each, merge in O(n)

problems.time_complexity: O(n log n) avg, O(n²) worst
problems.optimal_approach: Randomized pivot selection to avoid worst case

topics.topic_name: Solve Problems on Arrays [Easy → Medium → Hard]

problems.brute_force: Sort and return last element O(n log n)
problems.optimal_approach: Single traversal tracking max

problems.problem_name: Second Largest Element in an Array without Sorting
problems.brute_force: Sort and find O(n log n)
problems.optimal_approach: Two-pass: find max, then find max < max
Single pass checking arr[i] <= arr[i+1]
Two pointer: i slow, j fast; copy when arr[i] != arr[j]

problems.problem_name: Left Rotate Array by One Place
problems.brute_force: Store first element, shift all left, put at end
problems.optimal_approach: Same - O(n) is optimal
3-step reversal: reverse all, reverse first k, reverse rest

problems.brute_force: Extra array, place non-zeros first, fill rest with 0
problems.optimal_approach: Two pointer: slow tracks insertion point for non-zeros
Traverse array, return index when found

problems.problem_name: Find the Union of Two Sorted Arrays
problems.optimal_approach: Two pointer merge avoiding duplicates

problems.brute_force: Sort and check, or use extra array O(n)
problems.optimal_approach: Sum formula: n*(n+1)/2 - arraySum. Or XOR approach
Count current run, reset on 0, track max

problems.problem_name: Find Number that Appears Once (Others Twice)
problems.optimal_approach: XOR all elements; duplicates cancel, answer remains

problems.problem_name: Longest Subarray with Sum K (Positive Numbers)
problems.space_complexity: O(n) / O(1) sliding window for positives
problems.optimal_approach: Prefix sum with hashmap, or two pointer for all-positive
HashMap: for each element, check if (target-element) exists in map

problems.problem_name: Sort an Array of 0s, 1s and 2s (Dutch National Flag)
problems.brute_force: Merge sort or count frequencies, overwrite
problems.optimal_approach: Dutch National Flag: 3 pointers low=0, mid=0, high=n-1
Boyer-Moore Voting Algorithm
Kadane's: track current sum, reset to 0 if negative

problems.problem_name: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
problems.optimal_approach: Track min price seen, compute max profit at each step
Two pointers for pos/neg in result array, fill alternately

problems.brute_force: Find all permutations and pick next O(n! * n)
problems.optimal_approach: Find breakpoint from right, find successor, swap, reverse suffix

problems.brute_force: For each element check all to its right O(n²)
problems.optimal_approach: Traverse right to left tracking running max

problems.brute_force: Sort O(n log n) then scan, or nested loop O(n²)
problems.optimal_approach: HashSet, for each x where x-1 not in set, count streak

problems.brute_force: Mark -1 in first pass, set 0 in second O(n×m×(n+m))
problems.optimal_approach: Use first row and column as markers
4 boundary pointers (top, bottom, left, right), shrink inward
Prefix sum + HashMap: count(prefixSum - k)

problems.brute_force: Calculate each element using nCr O(n³)
problems.optimal_approach: Build row by row using previous row
Extended Boyer-Moore with 2 candidates
Sort + two pointer for inner two elements
Sort + two loops + two pointer for inner two
XOR prefix + HashMap similar to subarray sum = k
Sort by start, merge if current.start <= prev.end

problems.problem_name: Merge Two Sorted Arrays Without Extra Space
problems.brute_force: Copy into extra array and sort
problems.optimal_approach: Shell Sort gap method: compare with gap = ceil((n+m)/2)

problems.problem_name: Find the Missing and Repeating Number
problems.brute_force: Sort and scan, or use hash O(n) space
problems.optimal_approach: Math: (sum - expectedSum) and (sumOfSquares method), or XOR
Modified merge sort: count during merge when arr[l] > arr[r]
Modified merge sort: count pairs before merging
Track prefix and suffix products; handle negatives by considering both

problems.problem_name: Binary Search to Find X in Sorted Array
problems.optimal_approach: mid = (lo+hi)/2, eliminate half based on comparison
Binary search: find first index where arr[mid] >= target
Binary search: find first index where arr[mid] > target

problems.problem_name: Search in a Sorted Rotated Array
problems.optimal_approach: Identify sorted half, check if target is in it, search accordingly

problems.problem_name: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
problems.optimal_approach: Track minimum in sorted half, move to unsorted half
Move towards higher neighbor; peak is where both neighbors are smaller

problems.problem_name: Kth Smallest Element in BST / Find Kth Element in Sorted Matrix
problems.brute_force: Flatten and sort O(n² log n)
problems.optimal_approach: Binary search on answer: count elements <= mid

problems.problem_name: Find Square Root of a Number
problems.optimal_approach: Binary search between 1 and n for floor(√n)

problems.problem_name: Capacity to Ship Packages within D Days
problems.optimal_approach: Binary search on capacity: check if feasible to ship in D days

problems.brute_force: Try all speeds from 1 to max
problems.optimal_approach: Binary search on eating speed, check if all eaten in h hours
Sort + binary search on minimum distance, check if k cows can be placed
Binary search on max pages, check if m students can read
Binary search partition on smaller array

problems.brute_force: Split, reverse array of words, join
problems.optimal_approach: Two pointer to extract tokens, build result in reverse
Expand around center for each position O(n²), or Manacher's O(n)

problems.brute_force: Parse left to right with if-else
problems.optimal_approach: If current < next, subtract; else add
Handle whitespace, sign, digits, overflow carefully

problems.brute_force: Compare all strings character by character
problems.optimal_approach: Take first string as reference, compare with others

problems.brute_force: Sort both strings O(n log n)
problems.optimal_approach: Frequency array of 26 chars, increment for s, decrement for t
KMP on s + '#' + reverse(s)
Same simulation - no better approach

topics.topic_name: Learn LinkedList [Single, Double, Medium, Hard Problems]

problems.optimal_approach: Node class with data and next pointer

problems.problem_name: Delete Last Node of Linked List
problems.brute_force: Traverse to second-last and set next to null
problems.optimal_approach: Same O(n)

problems.problem_name: Find the Middle of Linked List
problems.brute_force: Count nodes, traverse to middle O(n) two passes
problems.optimal_approach: Fast-slow pointer: fast moves 2, slow moves 1
In-place: prev=null, curr=head, flip links

problems.problem_name: Detect a Cycle in Linked List
problems.brute_force: HashSet to store visited nodes O(n) space
problems.optimal_approach: Floyd's Tortoise and Hare algorithm

problems.problem_name: Find the Starting Point of a Cycle
problems.brute_force: HashSet to find first repeated node
problems.optimal_approach: After Floyd detects meeting point, reset one to head, advance both by 1 until they meet

problems.problem_name: Check if Linked List is Palindrome
problems.optimal_approach: Find mid, reverse second half, compare, restore
Two pointer: fast n ahead of slow; when fast reaches end, slow.next is the node

problems.problem_name: Add Two Numbers as Linked Lists
problems.brute_force: Extract numbers, add, convert back (overflow risk)
problems.optimal_approach: Traverse both simultaneously, handle carry

problems.brute_force: HashSet on one list, check other O(n+m) space
problems.optimal_approach: Two pointers switch to other list when reaching end

problems.brute_force: Extract to array, sort, rebuild O(n log n + n)
problems.optimal_approach: Merge sort on linked list using slow-fast pointer for mid
Merge sorted linked list column by column from right

problems.brute_force: Rotate one at a time k times
problems.optimal_approach: Make circular, find new tail at (len - k%len - 1), break
At each position, place 0 or 1 recursively
Swap-based or extra visited array
Backtracking with column, diagonal, anti-diagonal checks
Backtracking: try 1-9, check validity, recurse, undo
DFS from each cell, mark visited, backtrack
Include/Exclude pattern or bit manipulation for 2^n subsets

problems.space_complexity: O(k×x) where x = number of combinations
problems.optimal_approach: Backtracking: pick element (can repeat), or skip to next
Sort, skip duplicate elements at same level in recursion tree
Backtracking: check palindrome at each cut, recurse on rest
AND, OR, XOR, NOT, SHIFT operations

problems.problem_name: Check if ith Bit is Set

problems.optimal_approach: Brian Kernighan's: n &= (n-1) until 0, count iterations

problems.problem_name: Minimum Bit Flips to Convert Number
problems.optimal_approach: Count set bits in (start XOR goal)

problems.problem_name: Single Number II (appears 3 times, find once)
problems.optimal_approach: Count bits mod 3 for each bit position
XOR all → get xor of two nums → find rightmost set bit → partition into two groups

topics.topic_name: Stack and Queues [Learning, Pre-In-Post-fix, Monotonic Stack, Implementation]

problems.optimal_approach: Array with top pointer
Circular array with front and rear pointers

problems.brute_force: Count approach (only works for one type)
problems.optimal_approach: Stack: push open brackets, pop and check on close
Single queue: after push, rotate n-1 times
Single stack storing (val, currentMin) pairs
Monotonic decreasing stack: pop when greater found

problems.brute_force: For each bar find max left and right O(n²)
problems.optimal_approach: Two pointer: maintain left_max, right_max, add min-height water
Monotonic stack: find previous smaller and next smaller for each bar
Monotonic stack: for each element count subarrays where it's min

topics.topic_name: Sliding Window & Two Pointer Combined Problems

problems.optimal_approach: Sliding window with HashSet or HashMap tracking last index

problems.problem_name: Max Consecutive Ones III (with k flips)
problems.optimal_approach: Sliding window: shrink when zeros in window > k

problems.problem_name: Fruit Into Baskets (At Most Two Distinct Types)
problems.optimal_approach: Sliding window with map of at most 2 fruit types
Two pointer with need/formed counts and char frequency map
Binary heap with sift-up/sift-down operations

problems.problem_name: Kth Largest Element in an Array
problems.optimal_approach: Min-heap of size k; pop when size exceeds k
Max-heap of size k

problems.brute_force: Sort by frequency O(n log n)
problems.optimal_approach: Freq map + min-heap of size k

problems.time_complexity: O(log n) per insert, O(1) for median
problems.brute_force: Sort on every query O(n log n)
problems.optimal_approach: Two heaps: max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper half
Min-heap with (value, array_index, element_index)

problems.time_complexity: O(n log n + m log m)
problems.optimal_approach: Sort both, greedily assign smallest sufficient cookie
Track maxReach; if i > maxReach, return false
Greedy BFS: for each jump range, find max reachable next range

problems.brute_force: Add and sort O(n log n)
problems.optimal_approach: 3 phases: add non-overlapping before, merge overlapping, add rest
Sort by end, keep intervals with earliest end

problems.time_complexity: O(n log n + n × d) where d=max deadline
problems.optimal_approach: Sort by profit desc, greedily assign to latest available slot
Track $5 and $10 bills; greedily use $10 before $5 for $20 change

topics.topic_name: Binary Trees [Representations, Traversals, Medium and Hard Problems]

problems.optimal_approach: TreeNode class with left and right pointers
Iterative with explicit stack or Morris traversal
BFS with queue, process level by level
DFS: 1 + max(left height, right height)

problems.brute_force: For each node compute height of left+right O(n²)
problems.optimal_approach: Post-order DFS: track max diameter through each node

problems.problem_name: Check if Binary Tree is Balanced
problems.brute_force: Compute height at each node O(n²)
problems.optimal_approach: Return -1 for unbalanced subtree in bottom-up DFS

problems.problem_name: Lowest Common Ancestor of Binary Tree
problems.brute_force: Find paths to both nodes, find last common node
problems.optimal_approach: Post-order DFS: if node = p or q, return it; LCA is where both subtrees found
Post-order: for each node, update global max with left+right+node; return max single-branch gain
BFS with null markers for serialization; BFS reconstruction for deserialization
Compare with root: go left if smaller, right if larger
Traverse to appropriate null position and insert

problems.brute_force: Inorder traversal check O(n) but needs extra array
problems.optimal_approach: DFS with (min, max) range validation at each node
Inorder traversal counting k nodes, stop at k-th
Use BST property: if both in left go left, if both right go right, else root is LCA

problems.brute_force: Collect inorder list ahead of time O(n) space
problems.optimal_approach: Push left spine, pop gives next, push new left spine
3 cases: no child, one child, two children (replace with inorder successor)
Queue-based level-order traversal with visited array
Recursive/stack DFS with visited array
DFS/BFS to mark connected 1s, count starts
DFS from starting cell, change color, recurse on neighbors
BFS/DFS: if neighbor is visited and not parent, cycle exists
DFS with two visited arrays (visited + inCurrentPath); or Kahn's topological sort
DFS finish-order push to stack, or Kahn's BFS with in-degree
BFS level-by-level gives shortest path in unweighted graph
Priority queue (min-heap) + relaxation of edges
Relax all edges V-1 times; detects negative cycles in V-th iteration

problems.brute_force: Dijkstra from each vertex O(V² log V)
problems.optimal_approach: DP: dist[i][j] = min(dist[i][j], dist[i][k] + dist[k][j]) for each k
Greedy: always pick minimum weight edge crossing the cut
Sort edges, use Disjoint Set Union to add edges without cycles
Union by rank/size with path compression
Kahn's algorithm: if topoSort size < n, cycle exists

problems.problem_name: Introduction to DP - Fibonacci with Memoization
problems.optimal_approach: Top-down memoization or bottom-up tabulation
dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]; same as Fibonacci
dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i])

problems.brute_force: Try all subsets avoiding both first and last
problems.optimal_approach: Run house robber twice: [0..n-2] and [1..n-1], take max
dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1] or combinatorics nCr
dp[i][j] = grid[i][j] + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1])

problems.problem_name: Triangle (Min Path Sum from Top to Bottom)
problems.optimal_approach: Bottom-up DP: from row n-1, dp[j] = triangle[i][j] + min(dp[j], dp[j+1])
dp[i][j] = 1+dp[i-1][j-1] if match, else max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1])
DP O(n²) or patience sorting with binary search O(n log n)
dp[i][w] = max(dp[i-1][w], val[i] + dp[i-1][w-wt[i]])
dp[i] = min coins to make amount i; unbounded knapsack
If chars match: dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]; else 1+min(insert, delete, replace)

problems.problem_name: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II (Multiple transactions)
problems.optimal_approach: Greedy: sum all positive differences (price[i]-price[i-1])

problems.problem_name: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock with Cooldown
problems.optimal_approach: State machine DP: hold, sold, rest states

problems.time_complexity: O(L) per operation where L = word length
problems.optimal_approach: 26-ary tree where each node represents a character
Insert all suffixes into Trie, count total nodes
Insert all nums in binary Trie; for each num, greedily pick opposite bit
Build LPS array for pattern, use it to skip comparisons
Z-array: length of longest substring starting from i that is also a prefix
Doubly linked list + HashMap; move to front on access, remove tail on eviction
Two HashMaps + min-frequency tracker + DLL for each frequency
